name: action
description: Actions bring together recipes as functions to make them into one. Additionally, the allow for a new name, and more importantly, a transformer to be added, along with EITHER a recipe or a system_function.

name: automation_matrix
description: Cognition Matrices 1. Linear Cognition Matrix (agent_crew) 2. Branching Cognition Matrix (agent_mixture) 3. Hybrid Cognition Matrix (workflow) 4. Guided Cognition Matrix (conductor) 5. Probabilistic Cognition Matrix (monte_carlo) 6. Accelerated Cognition Matrix (hypercluster) 7. Immersive Cognition Matrix (the_matrix) 8. Evolving Cognition Matrix (knowledge_matrix)

name: broker
description: Dynamic value and message carriers throughout the application and especially in recipes and workflows

name: data_input_component
description: Official UI component for displaying brokers

name: display_option
description: A type of display, page or component where recipe results or other results are shown dynamically.

name: registered_function
description: Official system functions that should not be seen by users without a wrapper so we don't expose our actual function details.

name: system_function
description: Function wrapper that is publicly viewable by users and always used for all frontend presentations of 'function'

name: tool
description: Functions and tools available for AI Models to execute: These can be hosted within AME or elsewhere via an api endpoint or local access

name: transformer
description: A class of methods that can either transform data or change brokers or both, resulting in data bridging simple data gaps in the automation matrix.

name: view_registered_function
view_definition:
SELECT rf.id,
    rf.name,
    rf.module_path,
    rf.class_name,
    rf.description,
    rf.return_broker,
    COALESCE(json_build_object('id', b.id, 'name', b.name), '{}'::json) AS return_brokers,
    COALESCE(json_agg(json_build_object('id', a.id, 'name', a.name)) FILTER (WHERE (a.id IS NOT NULL)), '[]'::json) AS args,
    COALESCE(json_agg(json_build_object('id', sf.id, 'name', sf.name)) FILTER (WHERE (sf.id IS NOT NULL)), '[]'::json) AS system_functions
   FROM (((registered_function rf
     LEFT JOIN broker b ON ((rf.return_broker = b.id)))
     LEFT JOIN arg a ON ((a.registered_function = rf.id)))
     LEFT JOIN system_function sf ON ((sf.rf_id = rf.id)))
  GROUP BY rf.id, b.id, b.name;

name: view_registered_function_all_rels
view_definition:
SELECT rf.id,
    rf.name,
    rf.module_path,
    rf.class_name,
    rf.description,
    rf.return_broker,
    b.id AS broker_id,
    b.additional_params AS broker_additional_params,
    b.custom_source_component AS broker_custom_source_component,
    b.data_type AS broker_data_type,
    b.default_destination AS broker_default_destination,
    b.default_source AS broker_default_source,
    b.description AS broker_description,
    b.display_name AS broker_display_name,
    b.name AS broker_name,
    b.other_source_params AS broker_other_source_params,
    b.output_component AS broker_output_component,
    b.ready AS broker_ready,
    b.sample_entries AS broker_sample_entries,
    b.tags AS broker_tags,
    b.tooltip AS broker_tooltip,
    b.validation_rules AS broker_validation_rules,
    b.value AS broker_value,
    json_agg(json_build_object('id', a.id, 'data_type', a.data_type, 'default', a."default", 'name', a.name, 'ready', a.ready, 'required', a.required)) AS args,
    json_agg(json_build_object('id', sf.id, 'description', sf.description, 'input_params', sf.input_params, 'name', sf.name, 'output_options', sf.output_options, 'sample', sf.sample)) AS system_functions
   FROM (((registered_function rf
     LEFT JOIN broker b ON ((rf.return_broker = b.id)))
     LEFT JOIN arg a ON ((a.registered_function = rf.id)))
     LEFT JOIN system_function sf ON ((sf.rf_id = rf.id)))
  GROUP BY rf.id, b.id;
